metadata.description: Golden evaluation dataset for Healthcare RAG - Ask My Docs
metadata.notes: Expand to 50-200 pairs before production evaluation. Each entry must have a human-verified ground_truth answer from the source document.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended first-line treatment for uncomplicated hypertension in adults?
qa_pairs.ground_truth: According to clinical guidelines, lifestyle modifications including dietary changes (DASH diet), sodium restriction, weight loss, and increased physical activity are the first-line interventions. If lifestyle changes are insufficient, thiazide diuretics, ACE inhibitors, angiotensin receptor blockers (ARBs), or calcium channel blockers are recommended as initial pharmacological therapy.

qa_pairs.question: What are the WHO criteria for diagnosing Type 2 diabetes mellitus?
qa_pairs.ground_truth: The WHO criteria for diagnosing Type 2 diabetes include: fasting plasma glucose ≥7.0 mmol/L (126 mg/dL), 2-hour plasma glucose ≥11.1 mmol/L (200 mg/dL) during an oral glucose tolerance test, HbA1c ≥48 mmol/mol (6.5%), or a random plasma glucose ≥11.1 mmol/L with classic symptoms of hyperglycemia.

qa_pairs.question: What are the recommended childhood immunization schedules according to CDC guidelines?
qa_pairs.ground_truth: The CDC recommended immunization schedule for children includes: Hepatitis B at birth, 1-2 months, and 6-18 months; DTaP at 2, 4, 6 months, 15-18 months, and 4-6 years; MMR at 12-15 months and 4-6 years; Varicella at 12-15 months and 4-6 years; and annual influenza vaccine starting at 6 months of age, among others.

qa_pairs.question: What is the NIH-recommended treatment protocol for community-acquired pneumonia in hospitalized adults?
qa_pairs.ground_truth: For hospitalized adults with community-acquired pneumonia (CAP), the NIH/IDSA guidelines recommend empiric antibiotic therapy. Non-ICU patients should receive a beta-lactam plus a macrolide, or a respiratory fluoroquinolone monotherapy. ICU patients require a beta-lactam plus either azithromycin or a respiratory fluoroquinolone. Duration is typically 5-7 days for uncomplicated CAP.

qa_pairs.question: What are the global mortality rates for cardiovascular disease according to WHO?
qa_pairs.ground_truth: According to WHO data, cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) are the leading cause of death globally, accounting for approximately 17.9 million deaths per year, representing 32% of all global deaths. Of these, 85% are due to heart attack and stroke. Over three quarters of CVD deaths occur in low- and middle-income countries.
